us-politics.headline: US-Iran Talks Collapse as Tensions Rise in Middle East
us-politics.body:
Diplomatic efforts to negotiate with Iran ended in stalemate after 21 hours of intense negotiations. Senator J.D. Vance reported no progress in ceasefire talks, calling it bad news for Iran. Meanwhile, a new report suggests China is preparing to deliver advanced air defense systems to Iran - a move that could further complicate regional dynamics.

The talks, held in Pakistan, highlight the fragile state of US-Iran relations. Military preparations continue, with US forces already beginning to remove mines from the Strait of Hormuz - a critical maritime chokepoint.

sources.title: Vance says US-Iran talks end without deal after 21 hours of negotiations
Vance says no headway in ceasefire talks: ‘I think that’s bad news for Iran’
China preparing delivery of new air defense systems to Iran, report says
Sunday shows preview: Ceasefire on shaky ground as US, Iran hold direct talks in Pakistan
US military starts removing mines from Strait of Hormuz

us-politics.headline: Swalwell Faces Mounting Pressure Over Sexual Assault Allegations
us-politics.body:
Democratic Congressman Eric Swalwell confronts serious legal and political challenges after multiple allegations of sexual misconduct. The Manhattan District Attorney has confirmed an investigation into the claims. House Republicans are preparing a motion to remove Swalwell from Congress.

Additional scrutiny emerged from a Fox News report alleging Swalwell improperly paid a campaign nanny who lacked work authorization. Swalwell's staff have publicly stood with his accusers, signaling potential internal party fractures.

sources.title: Eric Swalwell accused of paying nanny with campaign funds while she lacked work authorization
Swalwell staffers stand with accusers as Manhattan DA confirms investigation
Manhattan DA to investigate Swalwell over sexual assault allegation
House Republican plans motion to oust Swalwell from Congress amid sexual assault allegations

us-politics.headline: White House Construction Battle Continues in Federal Courts
us-politics.body:
A federal appeals court has temporarily allowed White House ballroom construction to proceed, delivering a partial victory to the current administration. The ruling comes amid ongoing legal challenges and political controversy surrounding the project.

Former President Trump has simultaneously touted new plans for a Washington DC architectural project - a triumphal arch - further highlighting the ongoing tensions between competing political visions for the nation's capital.

sources.title: White House ballroom construction can continue for now, appeals court says
White House ballroom construction can continue, federal appeals court says
Trump touts newly released plans for D.C. triumphal arch
Federal judges say White House ballroom construction can continue, for now

au-politics.headline: Labor Weighs Household Relief Amid Global Tensions
au-politics.body:
The Albanese government is considering new economic support measures as global conflicts create financial pressure. Foreign Minister Penny Wong expressed disappointment over failed US-Iran peace talks, signaling potential long-term economic impacts.

Treasury is exploring relief options to help Australian households manage rising costs. Treasurer Jim Chalmers warned of increasingly polarised political dynamics, suggesting the economic challenges could reshape national political discourse.

sources.title: Australia news live: Wong says it’s ‘disappointing’ peace talks between US and Iran failed; Chalmers warns of ‘more polarising politics’
New relief for households being considered as Albanese government warns of ‘long tail’ from Iran war
No work, no savings, no way to wait: India's migrant workers head home

au-politics.headline: Western Australia Confronts Fuel Supply and Infrastructure Challenges
au-politics.body:
Western Australia is exploring a separate diesel stockpile to address potential fuel shortages. The state government is examining strategic reserves as a contingency against supply disruptions.

Additional infrastructure challenges emerged with reports of caravan park residents being evicted after ownership changes. These developments highlight ongoing economic and housing pressures in the state.

sources.title: WA considering separate stockpile of diesel amid fuel shortages
WA caravan park residents evicted after change of ownership
Minister casts doubt on road user tax due to EV uptake concerns

world.headline: US-Iran Talks Collapse as Tensions Escalate in Middle East
world.body:
Diplomatic efforts between the United States and Iran have broken down, with US Vice President JD Vance declaring no deal reached after intense negotiations in Pakistan. The talks ended in a stalemate, with Vance claiming the US has made its best and final offer.

Tensions continue to simmer, with the UN watchdog warning Iran against charging tolls in the Strait of Hormuz. US naval ships have already transited the strategic waterway, signaling continued military preparedness in the region.

sources.title: Watch JD Vance’s full remarks after US-Iran talks end without deal
US delegation leaves Pakistan without reaching Iran deal
Iran must not charge tolls in Strait of Hormuz, UN watchdog chief says
Iran war live: Vance says no deal reached, US has made ‘best, final offer’
Iran’s deputy FM says Tehran has ‘upper hand’ in talks with US
US says two naval ships ‘transited’ Strait of Hormuz for mine-clearing

world.headline: Ukraine Marks Grim Easter as War Grinds On
world.body:
Ukraine enters another Easter season under the shadow of prolonged conflict, with hopes for peace remaining elusive. The ongoing war continues to drain national morale and resources, despite international calls for a resolution.

The conflict shows no signs of immediate resolution, with both sides seemingly entrenched in their positions. Civilians bear the brunt of the continued hostilities, yearning for an end to the destructive stalemate.

sources.title: 'We need real peace': Easter truce fails to lift grim mood in war-torn Ukraine
Pope Leo urges world leaders to reject war and negotiate peace

world.headline: Israel Expands Settlements, Tensions Escalate with Palestinians
world.body:
Israel has approved new settlements in the occupied West Bank, drawing sharp condemnation from Palestinians and international observers. Prime Minister Netanyahu's rhetoric remains uncompromising - recently claiming Israel has strangled its opponents and intends to continue doing so.

Diplomatic tensions have further heightened, with Israel reprimanding a Spanish diplomat over the symbolic detonation of a Netanyahu effigy. The move underscores the increasingly fraught international relations surrounding the ongoing conflict.

sources.title: Netanyahu next to Middle East map: ‘We strangled them and have more to do’
Palestinians appalled as Israel approves settlements in occupied West Bank
Israel reprimands Spanish diplomat over detonation of Netanyahu effigy
Has Israeli society become conditioned to permanent war?
Israeli strikes kill at least 18 people across southern Lebanon

ai.headline: AI Breakthroughs Crack Agent Performance Benchmarks
ai.body:
Researchers at Berkeley's Research and Development Institute have claimed a significant breakthrough in AI agent performance testing. Their latest work suggests small AI models can now identify vulnerabilities previously thought detectable only by advanced systems - challenging existing assumptions about machine intelligence capabilities.

The findings hint at a potential shift in how we evaluate artificial intelligence. By demonstrating that less complex models can match sophisticated counterparts in critical detection tasks, the team has opened new conversations about AI scalability and intelligent system design.

sources.title: How We Broke Top AI Agent Benchmarks: And What Comes Next
Small models also found the vulnerabilities that Mythos found
AI models are terrible at betting on soccer—especially xAI Grok

ai.headline: Brain-Computer Interface Lets ALS Patient Dance Through Thought
ai.body:
A groundbreaking neural interface has enabled a dancer with ALS to perform live using only brainwave signals. The technology translates neural activity directly into movement - allowing artistic expression despite severe physical limitations.

This breakthrough represents more than a medical innovation. It signals a profound expansion of human-machine interaction, where neural signals can bridge seemingly impossible gaps between intention and action. The performance challenges traditional understanding of disability and technological potential.

sources.title: How a dancer with ALS used brainwaves to perform live
447 TB/cm² at zero retention energy – atomic-scale memory on fluorographane

btc.headline: Bitcoin Tumbles as US-Iran Tensions Spark Market Selloff
btc.body: Bitcoin prices dropped sharply after diplomatic negotiations between the United States and Iran failed to resolve escalating geopolitical tensions. The cryptocurrency market experienced significant volatility, with traders responding to the potential for increased global conflict. Market sentiment turned bearish as investors sought safer assets amid the uncertain international landscape.

sources.title: Bitcoin and other cryptos fall as U.S., Iranian negotiators fail to reach war resolution

btc.headline: Bitcoin Shows Signs of Market Stabilization Despite Volatility
btc.body: Bitcoin traders are detecting potential market stabilization as seller activity declines and short-term indicators suggest a possible trend reversal. Open interest in Bitcoin derivatives has approached $25 billion, signaling growing institutional engagement. Realized losses are decreasing - a potential signal that market participants may be reaching a point of capitulation and price equilibrium.

sources.title: Bitcoin price analysis sees new short squeeze as open interest nears $25B
Bitcoin signals potential seller exhaustion as realized losses decline

btc.headline: Musk's SpaceX Maintains Substantial Bitcoin Holdings
btc.body: SpaceX continues to hold $603 million in Bitcoin despite experiencing a $5 billion loss related to Elon Musk's xAI venture. The sustained cryptocurrency position suggests ongoing confidence in digital assets as a strategic investment, even amid broader financial challenges. The tech company's commitment to Bitcoin remains unwaveringly consistent with Musk's long-standing interest in cryptocurrency.

sources.title: Musk’s SpaceX holds $603 million in bitcoin despite $5 billion loss stemming from xAI
